curation_policy.highest_supported_stage: Rows are placed in the highest maturity stage supported by public evidence.
curation_policy.human_vs_veterinary: Human and veterinary indications are separated in notes; veterinary licensure is not human licensure.
curation_policy.gap_definition: Gap lanes mean no public pathway was identified from configured sources, not proof of unpublished absence.
curation_policy.auto_update_scope: Automation refreshes registry metadata, source availability, source titles, and review flags; it does not silently promote curated stages without human review.

stages.label: No dedicated clinical pathway
stages.description: No public target-specific vaccine pathway found; source-backed gap lane, not evidence of unpublished absence.

stages.label: Discovery / surveillance
stages.description: Virus discovery, receptor/tropism, taxonomy, or risk-characterization work without a vaccine candidate.

stages.label: Preclinical efficacy / platform evidence
stages.description: Animal or platform evidence, including challenge-model protection, without verified human clinical entry.

stages.label: Phase 1 / first-in-human
stages.description: First-in-human or early clinical development publicly scheduled, registered, or underway.

stages.label: Phase 1 complete / immune-bridge work
stages.description: Phase 1 completed or published; next work is dose selection, bridging, or affected-area development.

stages.label: Phase 2 / affected-area trial
stages.description: Mid-stage or affected-area clinical testing is funded, planned, registered, or launched.

stages.label: Reserve / emergency-use readiness
stages.description: Investigational reserve or outbreak-readiness manufacturing exists, distinct from licensure.

stages.label: Licensed / One Health deployment
stages.description: Licensed/deployed product in its stated indication; veterinary rows are not human licensure.

records.sponsor_or_steward: University of Oxford, CEPI, icddr,b, Serum Institute of India
records.setting: Bangladesh Phase IIa trial; investigational ready reserve stored by SII
records.status_summary: World-first Phase II Nipah vaccine trial launched in Bangladesh; SII reserve manufacturing planned for up to 100,000 investigational doses that could be used under emergency-use conditions.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Phase II safety/immunogenicity readout, continued assay/correlate development, and reserve governance for outbreak deployment.
records.curation_note: Mapped above Phase II because a public investigational reserve manufacturing commitment is part of the same development path; this is not licensure.

sources.title: CEPI Phase II launch; CEPI investigational reserve

registry_statuses.note: No structured API adapter configured; source page is monitored through source_checks.

records.platform: Live attenuated recombinant vesicular stomatitis virus vector expressing NiV-B G and Ebola GP
records.sponsor_or_steward: Public Health Vaccines, CEPI, NIAID originator technology
records.setting: Phase II Bangladesh trial funded/planned; stockpile option if successful and approved
records.status_summary: CEPI announced US$17.3M to support mid-stage testing in Bangladesh after Phase Ia/Ib safety and immunogenicity; the planned study targets about 500 adults and 75 paediatric participants.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Verify launch/recruitment status, then Phase II safety, tolerability, manufacturability, and immunogenicity data.
records.evidence_class: Phase II funded/planned; Phase I/IB complete
records.curation_note: Public sources checked support “Phase II funded/planned”; the dashboard does not claim a verified Phase II recruitment start.
records.lineage_or_scope: Bangladesh strain glycoprotein; potentially single-dose rVSV candidate

sources.title: CEPI PHV02 mid-stage trial announcement; PHV product page; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05178901/NCT06221813

registry_statuses.brief_title: A Phase 1 Study to Evaluate Safety & Immunogenicity of RVSV-Nipah Virus Vaccine Candidate PHV02 in Healthy Adult Subjects
registry_statuses.official_title: A Phase 1 Randomized, Single Center, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Dose-Response and Open-Label or Single Blind Booster Study to Evaluate the Safety and Immunogenicity of RVSV-Nipah Virus Vaccine Candidate PHV02 in Healthy Adult Subjects
registry_statuses.brief_summary: A Phase 1 Study to Evaluate the Safety and Immunogenicity of rVSV-Nipah Virus Vaccine Candidate PHV02 in Healthy Adult Subjects

registry_statuses.brief_title: Study to Evaluate Safety and Immunogenicity of a Prime-Boost Regimen of rVSV-Nipah Virus Vaccine Candidate PHV02 in Healthy Adult Subjects
registry_statuses.official_title: A Phase 1b Randomized, Observer-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Safety and Immunogenicity of a Prime-Boost Regimen of Three Dose Levels of PHV02, a Nipah Virus Vaccine Candidate (rVSV-ΔG-EBOV GP-NiVG) in Healthy Adults
registry_statuses.brief_summary:
The goal of this clinical trial is to test the safety and immunogenicity of PHV02 live, attenuated recombinant vesicular stomatitis virus vaccine expressing the Nipah Virus glycoprotein in healthy adult subjects. The main questions it aims to answer are:

* which doses of PHV02 are safe to administer to and well-tolerated by healthy adult subjects as a 2 dose regimen given 1 month apart?
* what is the immunologic response (Nipah-specific IgG ELISA antibody and neutralizing antibodies) to each dose level after a 2-dose regimen given 1 month apart? Participants will receive 2 intramuscular injections of PHV02 (2x105, 2x106, and 2x107 plaque-forming units \[pfu\]) or placebo on Day 1 and Day 29 and will be followed for 197 days.

review_flags.message: NCT05178901 reports COMPLETED; review whether the curated stage still describes the public pathway.
NCT06221813 reports COMPLETED; review whether the curated stage still describes the public pathway.

records.platform: Recombinant soluble Hendra virus G glycoprotein subunit with alum adjuvant
records.sponsor_or_steward: Auro Vaccines, PATH, CEPI; original USU/HJF technology
records.setting: US Phase 1 completed/published; potential reactive outbreak-control use case
records.status_summary: Phase 1 adult study is completed and published; reported interpretation was that tested doses/regimens were safe and immunogenic, with preclinical rationale for cross-protection across Nipah and Hendra.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Define dose/regimen, immune correlates, and affected-area or immune-bridging development pathway.
records.curation_note: Shown under Nipah clinical development because the human trial indication is Nipah; also tagged to Hendra because the antigen and animal-protection rationale are Hendra/Nipah cross-protective.
records.lineage_or_scope: Hendra soluble G antigen evaluated as a Nipah vaccine; preclinical protection against NiV and HeV

sources.title: CEPI first Phase 1 announcement; Lancet/PubMed phase 1 record; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT04199169; npj Vaccines single-dose NHP evidence

registry_statuses.brief_title: Safety and Immunogenicity of a Nipah Virus Vaccine
registry_statuses.official_title: A Phase 1 Randomized, Placebo-controlled, Observer-blind Trial to Assess the Safety and Immunogenicity of a Nipah Vaccine, HeV-sG-V (Hendra Virus Soluble Glycoprotein Vaccine), in Healthy Adults
registry_statuses.brief_summary: A first-in-human, phase 1 trial is to be conducted in a healthy adult population in the US to assess the safety and immunogenicity of three ascending Nipah vaccine (HeV-sG-V; Hendra virus soluble glycoprotein vaccine) dosages. Different dosing regimens and number of doses will also be explored.

records.platform: Lipid nanoparticle mRNA encoding chimeric pre-fusion F linked to G
records.status_summary: A 2026 Nature Medicine Phase 1 report found the vaccine safe and well tolerated in 40 adults, with no safety-related pauses or serious adverse events reported.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Larger endemic-region cohorts, dose/regimen selection, live-virus neutralization/correlate work, and development-pathway decision.
records.curation_note: Mapped to Phase 1 complete because peer-reviewed first-in-human results were publicly available at build time.
records.lineage_or_scope: NiV-M chimeric pre-fusion F/G design; potential cross-reactive henipavirus approach

sources.title: Nature Medicine phase 1 report; NIH launch announcement; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05398796

registry_statuses.brief_title: Dose Escalation, Open-Label Clinical Trial to Evaluate Safety, Tolerability and Immunogenicity of a Nipah Virus (NiV) mRNA Vaccine, mRNA-1215, in Healthy Adults
registry_statuses.official_title: VRC 322/DMID 21-0016: A Phase I, Dose Escalation, Open-Label Clinical Trial to Evaluate Safety, Tolerability and Immunogenicity of a Nipah Virus (NiV) mRNA Vaccine, mRNA-1215, in Healthy Adults
registry_statuses.brief_summary:
Background:

Nipah virus (NiV) is transmitted from animals to humans, from humans to humans, and through contaminated food. Infected people may have a cough and trouble breathing. Some people may develop serious symptoms, such as brain infection and inflammation, that can lead to death. There are no drugs or vaccines to treat or prevent NiV infection.

Objective:

To test the safety of an experimental vaccine (mRNA-1215) for NiV. Researchers will also evaluate how participants bodies respond to the vaccine.

Eligibility:

Healthy, nonpregnant adults aged 18 to 60 years.

Design:

Participants visited the NIH clinic 13 to 15 times over 14 to 16 months.

Participants received 2 doses of the experimental vaccine at 1 month apart. The vaccine was given as a shot into the muscle of the upper arm. Participants stayed in the clinic at least 30 minutes after each vaccination.

Participants were given a diary card and a thermometer. They recorded their temperature and any other reactogenicity symptoms for 7 days after each vaccination.

During each follow-up visit, 3 to 14 tubes of blood were drawn for research.

Some participants underwent an optional procedure called apheresis. A needle is placed into a vein in each arm. Blood is removed through one needle. The blood passed through a machine that separates some of the blood cells. The rest of the blood is returned to the body through another needle.

The mRNA-1215 vaccine cannot cause NiV infection.
registry_statuses.lead_sponsor: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)

records.sponsor_or_steward: University of Tokyo, European Vaccine Initiative, SCARDA; prior CEPI support
records.setting: Belgium first-in-human study scheduled for April 2026; Bangladesh Phase 1/2 planned
records.status_summary: EVI announced support for first-in-human clinical development, with a Belgium Phase 1 trial scheduled to begin in April 2026 and later Phase 1/2 studies planned in Bangladesh.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Confirm first participant dosing/recruitment, then Phase 1 safety/immunogenicity readout.
records.curation_note: Early clinical status is based on public announcement and trial registry listing; recruitment/completion status should be rechecked before operational decisions.
records.lineage_or_scope: Recombinant measles vector expressing Nipah antigens

sources.title: EVI first-in-human trial announcement; EU CTIS public trial entry

registry_statuses.note: No structured API adapter configured; source page is monitored through source_checks.

records.platform: Humanized anti-CD40 antibody fused to conserved Nipah epitopes
records.setting: African green monkey preclinical challenge model
records.status_summary: Cell Reports Medicine reported immunogenicity and protective efficacy of a CD40-targeted multi-epitope Nipah vaccine in African green monkeys.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Translation package, manufacturability, toxicology, and decision on clinical-enabling investment.
records.curation_note: Included as a notable preclinical platform row; not a verified clinical development programme.
records.lineage_or_scope: Bangladesh-strain multi-epitope G/F/N design targeted to CD40 on antigen-presenting cells

sources.title: Cell Reports Medicine CD40.NiV; PubMed record

records.setting: Mouse immunogenicity and Syrian hamster lethal-challenge studies
records.status_summary: npj Vaccines reported complete protection in hamsters and faster/broader neutralizing responses against NiV-Malaysia, NiV-Bangladesh, and Hendra pseudovirus/assays compared with soluble NiV G.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Reproducibility, NHP studies, manufacturability, and clinical-enabling package if prioritized.
records.curation_note: Included to show preclinical breadth and Hendra cross-neutralization signal; not a clinical product.
records.lineage_or_scope: Nipah G head domain displayed on self-assembling ferritin nanoparticles

records.virus: NiV, HeV, GhV, and historical MojV panel in mice
records.setting: Mouse immunogenicity / neutralization; not a clinical programme
records.status_summary: Preclinical work reported broad antibody responses with multi-antigen Fc-fusion constructs, while highlighting limited cross-protection between some henipa-like groups.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Update antigen panel to current ICTV taxonomy, test against current species/provisional viruses, and define whether a pan-henipavirus target product profile is feasible.
records.curation_note: Shown as broad preclinical concept. MojV has been reclassified into Parahenipavirus in current taxonomy, so this row is not counted as a core Henipavirus species pathway.
records.lineage_or_scope: Bivalent NiV/HeV and tetravalent G-protein Fc-fusion antigen designs

sources.title: Viruses/PMC Fc-based recombinant henipavirus vaccines; DOAJ summary

records.platform: Recombinant soluble Hendra virus G glycoprotein subunit
records.setting: Licensed veterinary vaccine for horses in Australia
records.status_summary: CSIRO and partners launched Equivac HeV in November 2012; APVMA full registration was announced in August 2015. It is a veterinary vaccine, not a licensed human Hendra vaccine.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Maintain uptake, update surveillance for Hendra variants, and clarify whether human Hendra indications need dedicated development.
records.curation_note: Placed in licensed lane because the veterinary product is licensed and deployed. It should not be interpreted as a licensed human vaccine.
records.lineage_or_scope: Horse vaccination to reduce equine disease and horse-to-human spillover risk

sources.title: CSIRO Hendra vaccine history; Zoetis APVMA registration release

records.platform: None publicly identified for a dedicated human Hendra label
records.status_summary: A veterinary horse vaccine exists; public sources checked did not identify a dedicated human Hendra vaccine indication advancing separately from cross-protective Nipah/Hendra candidates.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Clarify target product profile and regulatory path if human Hendra vaccination is prioritized; evaluate whether HeV-sG-V or other cross-protective candidates can support a Hendra indication.
records.evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
records.curation_note: Gap does not mean impossible or unnecessary; it means no separate human Hendra clinical path was identified from public sources.
records.lineage_or_scope: Humans occupationally exposed to horses/bats; human-label vaccine indication

sources.title: CSIRO Hendra vaccine history; CEPI HeV-sG-V cross-protective rationale; ICTV Henipavirus report

records.status_summary: Current public sources identify Cedar virus as a henipavirus species, but no dedicated vaccine development pathway or clinical programme was found.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Risk characterization, receptor/tropism work, animal-pathogenicity assessment, and only then a target product profile if warranted.
records.evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
records.curation_note: No pathway found is a source-based curation result, not proof that no unpublished work exists.

sources.title: ICTV Henipavirus report; species list search result

records.candidate: Ghanaian bat / Kumasi virus vaccine pathway
records.platform: None publicly identified as target-specific clinical pathway
records.status_summary: Ghanaian bat/Kumasi virus is in the current henipavirus species frame; public sources checked did not identify a target-specific clinical vaccine path.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Risk assessment, updated antigenic panel work, and decision on inclusion in broad/pan-henipavirus vaccine concepts.
records.evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
records.curation_note: Broad-platform data are captured separately; this row represents absence of a Ghanaian bat/Kumasi-specific clinical programme in public sources.

sources.title: ICTV Henipavirus report; Fc-based broad vaccine concept

records.status_summary: Angavokely virus was added to the henipavirus species frame; no dedicated vaccine programme or clinical path was found in public sources.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Risk characterization, receptor usage/pathogenicity work, and update broad-vaccine antigen panels if warranted.
records.evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
records.curation_note: Mapped as a gap because discovery/characterization exists but no vaccine development pathway was identified.

sources.title: ICTV current species-list result; JVI/PMC discovery paper

records.platform: No vaccine candidate; receptor/tropism/risk work only
records.sponsor_or_steward: CSIRO and collaborators / Emerging Infectious Diseases report
records.setting: Australian bat surveillance and in-vitro characterization
records.status_summary: A 2025 Emerging Infectious Diseases report described Salt Gully virus as a novel henipavirus from Australian pteropid bats, most closely related to Angavokely virus, with unknown human-disease risk.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Establish taxonomy status, receptor usage, host range, animal pathogenicity, and whether countermeasure inclusion is warranted.
records.curation_note: Included as a surveillance/gap lane rather than a core recognized species pathway.
records.lineage_or_scope: Pteropid bat isolate, Australia; closest to Angavokely in published analysis

sources.title: Emerging Infectious Diseases Salt Gully virus report
